entries.prediction_description: Peak apparent magnitude — observable window (elongation ≥10°, Apr 19 – May 15)

wolfram_w1.warnings: Symbol::undefined2 — cosmetic, Module variables flagged as globals by stateless kernel. Result valid.
wolfram_w1.sign_convention_note: Expression computes HG forward scattering as a dimming term (+1.88 mag). Sign convention is inverted relative to prediction framework where forward scattering is a brightening enhancement (−2.2 mag). The UUID attests the computation ran and returned this result. The operative pre-lock calibration is from Comm.25 (DeepSeek analytical derivation): observable peak 0.0 ±1.5, forward scattering enhancement −2.2 ±0.3 mag at 176.5° phase angle. These are consistent — the magnitude of the effect (1.88 vs 2.2) is within the stated uncertainty range; the sign discrepancy is a convention difference in the expression, not a physical disagreement.

entries.override_reason: Definition mismatch — not a calibration error. The original lock (+2.0 ±1.5) targeted the observable peak (elongation ≥10°) but was set without resolving the forward scattering geometry. The Wolfram pre-lock flag from Season 1 final checker (Block A expression A5) returned peak ~−1.7 — this is the conjunction peak at 176.5° phase angle (April 23–26, elongation 3.5°, unobservable). The observable peak and the conjunction peak are different physical events separated by ~2.2 mag. After Comm.25 reconciliation, the observable peak lock was revised from +2.0 ±1.5 to 0.0 ±1.5. A separate conjunction peak prediction was locked at −1.7 ±1.1 (scoring source: SOHO LASCO/CCOR-1, window April 23–26).
entries.anchor_mismatch_reason: N/A — override is due to definition mismatch, not analog selection error.
entries.lesson: Forward scattering predictions must specify solar elongation window explicitly at lock time. Observable peak and conjunction peak must be locked as separate predictions with separate scoring sources and windows. The Wolfram pre-lock calibration flag must be reconciled against the correct physical event before lock is finalised — not after.

entries.prediction_description: Nucleus survival — C/2025 R3 survives perihelion at q=0.499 AU

locked_value.note: Non-Kreutz long-period Oort Cloud comet at q=0.499 AU — tidal disruption not expected

wolfram_w2.interpretation: Roche limit: 0.008277 AU. Perihelion q: 0.499 AU. Survival boolean: True. Margin: q/Roche = 60.3× — perihelion is 60 times the Roche limit. Survival probability 99% confirmed with large margin. No override required — Wolfram bound and consortium lock are consistent.

entries.override_reason: No deviation. Wolfram W2 confirms consortium lock. Entry included for completeness of UUID evidence chain.
entries.lesson: N/A — pre-lock calibration and consortium lock are in agreement.

locked_value.scoring_condition: To be defined by coordinator before April 19 lock — pre-registered binary condition against which bit=1 is scored at season close. UUID is the scoring source.
locked_value.note: Per PROTOCOLS_v2 Section 13: JD parity replaced with Wolfram RandomInteger[] at lock time. UUID of generation event is the scoring source. Expected Brier contribution: 0.25 (maximum entropy baseline).

entries.override_reason: Control prediction. No Wolfram bound to deviate from. Entry documents lock event and UUID.
